actions.action: Retrieve available teacher avatar IDs using system tools or default mapping, and associate each news genre with a unique avatar ID.
actions.fallback_action: If avatar ID retrieval from the API fails, retry 3 times; if still unsuccessful, use the default avatar ID mapping provided for each genre.

recipe.steps: Attempt to retrieve teacher avatar IDs for the user by using approved internal tools or data storage. If not supported, proceed to fallback method.

If retrieval fails, retry the avatar ID retrieval process up to three times. If all attempts fail, switch to the fallback: assign a predefined avatar ID to each news genre according to the default mapping provided.
Save the finalized genre-to-avatar ID mapping for use in later actions, using the assistant's memory saving tool.

actions.action: Mark the current user's conv_id as the source conv_id for the 2-hour window.
actions.fallback_action: If marking the source conv_id fails, retry the marking action up to 2 times. If it still fails, check if another valid conv_id has already been marked within the same 2-hour window and use it as the source.

recipe.steps: Retrieve the current user's conv_id using the appropriate system tool.

Save the retrieved conv_id as the source conv_id, along with activation and expiry timestamps (2 hours from activation), using the system's data saving tool under the key source_conv_id.active_window.

actions.action: Assigned a unique avatar to each genre: Politics, Business, Sports, Breaking News, and Weather, using the genre-to-avatar ID mapping saved in memory.
actions.fallback_action: If the mapping is unavailable in memory, prompt the user for avatar IDs or apply the default mapping.

recipe.steps: Retrieve the genre-to-avatar ID mapping from memory where it was previously saved. If not found, use the default mapping or prompt for user input.
For each genre (Politics, Business, Sports, Breaking News, Weather), select its unique avatar ID from the mapping.

actions.action: Fetched the latest headlines for each genre (Politics, Business, Sports, Breaking News, and Weather) using search queries and generated a short narration script for each, suitable for news recaps.
actions.fallback_action: If search headline fetching is unavailable, prompt the user to input headlines for each genre.

recipe.steps: For each news genre (Politics, Business, Sports, Breaking News, Weather), initiate a search query to collect the latest headlines relevant to the topic from Google or available headline sources.
Summarize the latest headlines into a brief, easy-to-understand narration script. Format each like: 'Here’s your quick update on [genre name]: [headline details].'

actions.can_perform_without_user_input: no-I need either access to headline-fetching/search tools or the headlines must be provided by the user.

actions.action: Generate a realistic AI video for each genre (Politics, Business, Sports, Breaking News, Weather) using the assigned avatar and generated narration script.
actions.fallback_action: If video generation fails, retry video generation up to 3 times; if still unsuccessful, prompt the user for further action or consider alternate means of delivering the news update.

recipe.steps: For each genre, collect the corresponding narration script and the assigned avatar ID previously mapped.
Use the video generation tool to create one realistic AI video per genre by supplying the avatar and its corresponding script.

actions.action: Check the generation status of each AI video for the five news genres by querying the provided API endpoint using each video's conv_id, and repeat the check until all are marked as successful.
actions.fallback_action: If API checks fail, retry status checking up to three times; after repeated failures, prompt the user for next steps.

recipe.steps: For each genre video, retrieve its conv_id from memory or tracking system.
Query the API endpoint for each video's conv_id to check the status of video generation.
Repeat status queries at regular intervals until all videos are marked as 'success'.

actions.can_perform_without_user_input: no-API access for status-checking is not currently supported in this environment without user intervention or an available tool.

actions.action: Stored all 5 generated videos under the source conv_id, saving metadata including mapping of genre to avatar, video URL, generation timestamp, and expiry timestamp (set to 2 hours after generation).
actions.fallback_action: If saving metadata fails, retry saving up to three times; if the issue persists, notify the user for alternative instructions.

recipe.steps: Collect each video’s metadata: genre, avatar ID, video URL, generation timestamp, and compute an expiry timestamp set 2 hours after generation.
Bundle all 5 videos’ metadata under the source conv_id for organized future access.
Save the complete metadata record using the assistant’s memory saving tool.

actions.action: Marked the source conv_id as ready, indicating that all required videos and metadata have been successfully stored and the content block is finalized for delivery or access.
actions.fallback_action: If marking as ready fails, retry up to three times; if unsuccessful after retries, notify the user for manual resolution.

recipe.steps: Update the status of the source conv_id in memory or tracking system to 'ready' after verifying all required data is stored.
